summary: Real-run validation was attempted once. It failed with a genuine, previously-unknown bug (Vercel function timeout under real external network conditions), discovered before any state-persistence claim could be tested. Per this engagement's own standing rule, no fix-and-continue was attempted under this authorization -- flags were reverted immediately and this is reported as a STOP.

sequence_of_events.result: PASS -- baseline counters recorded (property_listings=558, listing_sources=563, property_clusters=419, property_cluster_members=419, discovery_candidates=7564, source_offer_observations=0, openserp_query_rotation_state=2718, openserp_engine_budget_state=3)

User-provided screenshot revealed the GitHub Actions workflow's schedule trigger (*/30 * * * *) IS live on the default branch (run #6, 'Triggered via schedule', Success) -- contradicting the earlier verbal confirmation that it was never active. User explicitly chose to accept the residual collision risk and proceed with a short flags-on window rather than disable the workflow first.

OPENSERP_AUTOMATED_INGESTION_ENABLED / OPENSERP_INGESTION_WRITE_ENABLED / OPENSERP_INGESTION_CRON_ENABLED set to true on Vercel Production, after explicit user confirmation that this specific flag (named CRON_ENABLED but functioning as the code's third write-authorization AND-gate, with no separate manual-only flag existing in the code) was understood and accepted.
dpl_DhpUhS5A7RVYgRSN7tMUUeb1fKDf, READY -- flags confirmed live (pulled and verified literal 'true' on all three).
User clicked 'Re-run jobs' on existing run #6 (not a fresh 'Run workflow' click) -- functionally equivalent (same HTTP call to the live endpoint), but not a strictly new workflow_dispatch event.

FAILED after 2m4s -- job 'Call the secured OpenSERP ingestion endpoint' returned HTTP 504 / FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT from Vercel's platform (not the app code itself). The batch (budget=12, first-ever real run) made real network calls to bing/duckduckgo/ecosia via the OpenSERP binary/HTTP path and collectively exceeded the route's maxDuration=120s.

Flags reverted to false on Vercel Production immediately upon discovering the failure; redeploy dpl_6wuUmsu9d7HcQgUMi32iMi5JnSx3 (READY) to make the revert live; verified all 3 flags literal 'false' via env pull; verified endpoint returns 401 with no secret (sanity).

openserp_query_rotation_state.note: 0 rows have last_run_id set -- the loop was killed before persistRotationUpdates() was ever reached

openserp_engine_budget_state.note: all 3 rows still show the original migration-seed updated_at timestamp -- persistBudgetState() was never reached either

discovery_candidates.cause: run-lock sentinel row (provider='openserp-ingestion-lock', run_id='openserp-cron-2026-07-18T17-46-28-731Z', locked_at='2026-07-18T17:46:28.736Z') inserted by acquireIngestionRunLock() at the very start of the request -- and never deleted, because Vercel's platform-level FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT kill does not run the route handler's `finally` block (which calls releaseIngestionRunLock()). This is a STUCK LOCK, still present as of this report.
discovery_candidates.impact_if_untouched: Any future ingestion attempt (real or NOOP-path) will fail to acquire the run lock via the same compare-and-set mechanism until this sentinel row is cleared -- this must be addressed before any future real-run attempt succeeds, but was NOT touched in this session (deleting/modifying discovery_candidates rows requires its own explicit authorization, not covered by this ODM).

root_cause_assessment.what_failed: Vercel's own platform-level function timeout (FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT), not application logic
root_cause_assessment.why: route.ts declares `export const maxDuration = 120;`. runIngestionCycle's batch loop calls runOpenSerpLiveQuery sequentially (up to 2 engines per query) for the full batch (12 queries at the default START_BUDGET=12) -- real network round-trips to real search engines apparently exceed 120s in aggregate under real conditions, something never observed in any prior test (all previous gates/tests used mocked or fixture SERP responses, never real network latency at this batch size).
root_cause_assessment.is_this_a_pre_existing_bug_or_introduced: Pre-existing in run-orchestrator.ts's batch-processing design (sequential per-query engine calls, no per-query or per-batch timeout budget, no early-return before the platform timeout) -- NOT introduced by OPENSERP-SERVERLESS-STATE-PERSISTENCE-1's changes (which only touched state persistence, not query execution timing/concurrency). This is the first time this code path has ever been exercised with write=true and a full real batch against Production.

not_yet_tested.cross_invocation_state_persistence: NOT VALIDATED against real Production/real network conditions -- the core purpose of this ODM was not reached because the run never got past the query-execution loop to the persistence step
not_yet_tested.second_workflow_dispatch_run: NOT PERFORMED -- stopped after the first attempt's failure, per this engagement's standing 'no fix-and-continue' rule

flags_final_state.verified_via: vercel env pull, literal values read back

outstanding_cleanup_required: Stuck run-lock sentinel row in discovery_candidates (id=3f9bb7b7-cf56-4322-ac35-8ee5cc9dd6bf) -- needs explicit authorization to delete before any future real run
GitHub Actions schedule trigger is live on the default branch (contrary to earlier assumption) -- needs an explicit decision (disable vs. accept) before any future flags-on window
recommended_next_step: A new, narrowly-scoped mission to fix the timeout risk (e.g., reduce the real-run batch size well below what fits in 120s, add a time budget check inside the batch loop to stop early and still persist partial progress, and/or raise maxDuration if the Vercel plan allows) before attempting OPENSERP-SERVERLESS-STATE-REAL-RUN-VALIDATION-1 again.
